rule_text: All items must be written in third person. Avoid use of 'I', 'us', 'our', 'we', 'my'. Replace with the organization name or neutral phrasing.
All submissions must be concise and clear. This is not the space to be witty or poetic.
Avoid exclamation marks in headlines and minimize them in body text. Use professional, neutral tone.
All event-related listings must include the time, date, and location of the event. If an activity is not free, a price must be included.
Use 'Today' and 'Tomorrow' instead of the specific date when appropriate. This applies to dates in both headlines and body text.
Embed hyperlinks in meaningful anchor text rather than displaying raw URLs. Use descriptive link text like 'Learn more', 'Register', 'Apply now'.
Use AP style for dates: abbreviate months with 6+ letters (Jan., Feb., Aug., Sept., Oct., Nov., Dec.). Spell out March, April, May, June, July. Use numerals for dates (Jan. 5, not January fifth).

Use AP style for times: lowercase a.m. and p.m. with periods. Use noon and midnight instead of 12 p.m. and 12 a.m. Use figures: 1 p.m., 3:30 p.m. Use a hyphen for same-period time ranges: '3-4 p.m.' Use 'from' with 'to' only when spanning a.m. to p.m.: 'from 9 a.m. to 3 p.m.'
Lengthy submissions should display with a few sentences in the newsletter and a link to a website containing the entire message. UCM reserves the right to trim lengthy submissions.
Items generally run once. They can run twice if there is an RSVP or registration component.
Items running more than once should not be resubmitted with altered text unless the original has an error.
Announcements should be chronological, with more important items at the top (e.g., university-wide announcements, urgent notices).
All submissions must be affiliated with the University of Idaho.
Thesis defenses are not included in the newsletter.
Sports competitions outside the Moscow-Pullman, Lewiston-Clarkston area are not included.
Limited-audience application deadlines and items intended for a small subset of the audience should be handled through other means.
Body text must be exactly one paragraph. Do not use bullet points, numbered lists, or multiple paragraphs.
Do not use bold, italics, or other text emphasis formatting in newsletter items.
Do not put quotation marks around event names in headlines.
Use single quotation marks for titles of works (books, films, etc.) in headlines.
Use double quotation marks for titles of works (books, films, etc.) in body text.
Do not italicize titles of works. Use quotation marks instead per the headline and body rules.
Do not use the Oxford comma (serial comma). In a list of three or more, omit the comma before 'and' or 'or'. Example: 'red, white and blue' not 'red, white, and blue'.
Hyphenate compound modifiers before a noun. Example: 'full-time employee' not 'full time employee'.
Use 'in' for rooms and buildings when describing event locations. Example: '3-4 p.m. Tuesday, Feb. 10, in TLC 245'.
Capitalize academic terms (Fall, Spring, Summer) only when a year follows. Example: 'Fall 2026' but 'this fall'.
Use Q&A formatting when the submission references a question-and-answer format.
Pair the day of the week with dates for events within the coming month. Events beyond 30 days out do not need a day of the week, only a date.
Events are assumed to be free unless otherwise noted. If an event has a cost, include the price. Do not write 'free' unless the original submission explicitly states it.
Do not include the year for events occurring within 12 months of the publication date, even if the event is in the next calendar year. Include the year only for events more than 12 months out.
Abbreviate 'University of Idaho' to 'U of I' on all references. Do not write out 'University of Idaho' repeatedly. Never use 'UI' alone as an abbreviation.
Order event details as: time, day, date, location. Example: '3-4 p.m. Wednesday, Feb. 12, in the Pitman Center Vandal Ballroom'.
Do not include 'PT' or 'Pacific time' unless the event is in another time zone such as Boise, Twin Falls or Idaho Falls. All events are assumed Pacific time by default.
Use em dashes — with spaces on both sides. Replace all en dashes with em dashes.
Format phone numbers as XXX-XXX-XXXX. Do not use parentheses around the area code.
Spell out acronyms on first reference and define key terms. Use the acronym in parentheses after the first spelled-out reference.
Flag language that could reasonably be read as Diversity, Equity and Inclusion-related for editor review, even if it may ultimately be acceptable.
